description_long: This object is a robust, upright guard stone made primarily of gray stone, featuring a roughly hewn, polygonal top and a rectangular base. A prominent reddish-brown band, resembling a metal or leather strap, encircles its midsection, providing both visual contrast and the suggestion of reinforcement. The upper face displays engraved or carved symbols and markings, some of which appear to be stylized numerals or letters, while the lower section also bears a line of abstract or ancient script. The stone surface is visibly textured, with a speckled, weathered appearance, indicating exposure to the elements. The edges and corners are slightly chipped and irregular, suggesting long-term use and occasional impact. This guard stone is designed to serve as a protective barrier, often found at the edge of streets or driveways to prevent vehicle encroachment.
description: A weathered gray stone guard stone with engraved markings and a reddish-brown band around its middle, featuring a rough, textured surface and signs of age.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main engraved face with abstract symbols and the reddish-brown band clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile of the stone and the full wrap of the reddish-brown band.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a different set of engraved markings and the same band, with the stone's rough texture evident.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the band, with minimal surface detail visible.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the edge of urban streets, driveways, or near the corners of sidewalks to protect infrastructure and prevent vehicle overrun.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be installed upright, partially embedded in the ground for stability, with the engraved face oriented toward oncoming traffic or pedestrians.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible chipping and smoothing on the edges and corners, likely caused by vehicle contact and foot traffic. The surface shows signs of weathering, such as discoloration and erosion, especially on the upper and exposed areas.
attribute_historical_markings: Engraved symbols and abstract script are present on both the upper and lower sections of the stone, with the front face featuring more prominent and stylized markings.
